title: Was the spec ops commander in EoE aware of SEELEs plan
display_title: Was the spec ops commander in EoE aware of SEELEs plan
wikitext:
[]: Was the spec ops commander in EoE aware of SEELE's plan?             Quote Post by Cypher » Fri Dec 11, 2009 8:41 am He doesn't look to worried and the communications officer behind him is smiling as Unit 01 and the MP eva's are beginning to generate the anti-at field. So was the commander of the invading special forces unit aware of SEELE's plan and sympathised with them?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Fri Dec 11, 2009 9:59 am I think you're reading too much into that; I would expect that that he was told that NERV was trying to instigate 3I (probably just told him "It's like 2I, only worse"), and that they (the army) need to prevent that by any means necessary, and he realizes here that the mission (the "prevent" part) has failed. Also: ASPECT RATIO!

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Dec 11, 2009 1:52 pm Shin-seiki's explanation is probably the best one at explaining both the army's orders and motivation. In fact, the very same story was spun to the Prime Minister earlier in #25'. And to be honest, we still don't know how large an element of truth there was to the charge that Gendou wanted to initiate Third Impact. I always too the commanders look and words as the resigned look of a professional facing death(The Captain in Xenogears anyone?). If Anno wanted to be more cliched, I suppose he could have had him take a puff of a cigar or something. I only spotted the second soldiers grin recently and I honestly have no idea what he's so happy about. Maybe he just likes the pretty light show. We're open to suggestions.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Dec 11, 2009 3:34 pm Of course they weren't. The Spec Ops were killing NERV because they were told that they planned to kill humanity. SEELE is...uh...planning to do WORSE. As for the smiling fucker, maybe he doesn't understand what's going on and thinks the Evas are exploding.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Fri Dec 11, 2009 6:16 pm its not a smiley, its just a monalisa effect.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Fri Dec 11, 2009 10:28 pm I always thought it was a nervous smile: these guys might be hardened soldiers, but they've probably never seen anything like the stuff that's going down in front of them...

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sat Dec 12, 2009 12:48 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Maybe he just likes the pretty light show.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 12, 2009 12:58 am Everybody else fielded the original question nicely, so I don't have much to add there. Though I somehow missed that officer's smile til now, my guess is it was a nervous smile/in awe of what was happening.

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Sat Dec 12, 2009 2:45 am I think the smiling guy is having a cliche "so this is end, huh?" type moments and he's looking back on the good times in his life. Maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Dec 12, 2009 11:44 am AVman9 wrote: I think the smiling guy is having a cliche "so this is end, huh?" type moments and he's looking back on the good times in his life. Maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sat Dec 12, 2009 12:03 pm Fixed? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Dec 12, 2009 1:22 pm Yes, it's very fitting in an amusing way.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sat Dec 12, 2009 2:01 pm Definitely not, though once the commander saw the Unit-01/Harpy clusterfuck, he knew it was over.

[]:             Quote Post by trident » Sun Dec 13, 2009 6:33 am Incidentally, it seems rather odd that by End of Evangelion, all pretense that the Second Impact was caused by a meteor is completely discarded.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Thu Dec 17, 2009 10:34 pm Smile guy probably thought the MP Eva's were performing their finishing move or something.
